finding: Runner-sync cleanup is not fully task-scoped after close: broker runner-sync release can reference a receipt that is not generated or committed by a governed post-close pathway, and foreign receipts such as .atm/history/evidence/ATM-GOV-0168.runner-sync-receipt.json can remain as untracked residue.
expectedBehavior: Each task that runs runner-sync should end with a clean worktree: build should emit a governed receipt, broker release should require or write that receipt through task evidence/event context, and taskflow close should either include release artifacts or provide a safe post-close runner-sync commit path.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0169 close on 2026-07-18, npm run build updated release/** and broker runner-sync release cleared steward work runner-sync-b58f23d1, but no ATM-GOV-0169.runner-sync-receipt.json was generated. A manual receipt was rejected by pre-commit with ATM_PROTECTED_STATE_EVIDENCE_FILE_MISSING_TASK_CONTEXT. Worktree also retained foreign untracked .atm/history/evidence/ATM-GOV-0168.runner-sync-receipt.json from cursor-gov-0168.
followUp: Open a GOV task to make runner-sync receipt publication first-class: emit receipt during sealed build, append a lane/task event, include it in task evidence context, and add a residue check that fails close if the active task leaves release or runner-sync artifacts uncommitted.
